title: helianthella parryi
intro: Helianthella parryi, also known as Parry's sunflower or mountain sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to create herbal preparations that can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as promote digestive health and reduce stress. The roots and leaves are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
